title: Doctor i18n migration — bootstrap/foundation batch (L2087-3034, ~26 sites)
description: Migrate the bootstrap/foundation region of doctor.ts (L2087-3034) from hardcoded English template literals to translator-driven keys. Region covers: bootstrap_anchor, bootstrap_marker_migration, L1/L2 bootstrap snapshot drift, knowledge_dir_missing, baseline_filename_format, forensic (scan_evidence), meta (agents_metadata), rule_content_refs, knowledge_test_index, event_ledger (+partial write), claude_mcp_config_in_wrong_file, meta_manually_diverged, knowledge_dir_unindexed, stable_id_collision, counter_desync, preexisting_root_files. Wire each `okCheck()` / `issueCheck()` call to use `t('doctor.check.<inspect_key>.name')` and `t('doctor.check.<inspect_key>.<arm>.{message|remediation|ok}', vars)`. Add the `t` parameter to runDoctorReport signature OR thread via a context object; choose path that minimizes signature churn (see implementation). DO NOT change the `code` field (3rd argument to issueCheck) — that's machine-readable contract. DO NOT add new keys to en.ts / zh-CN.ts in this task — that's TASK-05's job. Calls to `t()` will return key string verbatim until TASK-05 backfills, which is acceptable mid-wave and would be caught by TASK-05's snapshot test.

files.target: L2087-3034 (approximately 26 okCheck/issueCheck call sites). Specific anchors: bootstrap_anchor near L2299/2314, bootstrap_marker_migration near L2390-2419, L1/L2 drift near L2390-2441, baseline_filename L2410-2419, forensic L2431-2443, meta L2451-2472, rule_content_refs L2477-2504, knowledge_test_index L2509-2517, event_ledger L2522-2530, event_ledger_partial L2550-2562, mcp_config L2535-2545, knowledge_dir_unindexed L2707-2716, counter_desync L2860-2869, stable_id_collision L2878-2887, meta_manually_diverged L2893-2918, preexisting_root_files L3015-3034
files.change: For each call site: (1) thread translator `t` into the create*Check function (add as parameter, then update callers to pass it from runDoctorReport scope where t is constructed via getDoctorTranslator); (2) replace name string with `t('doctor.check.<key>.name')`; (3) replace ok message with `t('doctor.check.<key>.ok', vars?)`; (4) replace issue message with `t('doctor.check.<key>.<arm>.message', vars?)`; (5) replace remediation with `t('doctor.check.<key>.<arm>.remediation', vars?)`. Key naming convention: <inspect_key> matches the `code` field stem (e.g. `bootstrap_anchor`, `event_ledger_missing`, `event_ledger_not_writable`). For multi-arm checks (event_ledger has 3 arms missing/not_writable/invalid), each arm gets its own key suffix. Plural cases: use `.singular` and `.plural` key pairs per planning-context D2. Caller selects: `t(count === 1 ? 'doctor.check.X.message.singular' : 'doctor.check.X.message.plural', {count: String(count)})`.

files.target: runDoctorReport function signature (L808) and check construction site (L917+)
files.change: Add `translator: Translator` parameter to runDoctorReport (or accept locale and call createTranslator internally — choose minimum-churn path; threading translator is more flexible for testing). Pass translator down to every create*Check function. Import Translator type from @fenglimg/fabric-shared.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L900-1020 (runDoctorReport main body + checks array construction) to map exact call sites.
Read all 26 affected create*Check functions to enumerate every English string + placeholder. Maintain a working scratch list of (line, current_string, new_key, vars) for each.
Add `import { type Translator } from "@fenglimg/fabric-shared";` near existing imports if not present.
Modify runDoctorReport signature: `async function runDoctorReport(projectRoot: string, t: Translator): Promise<DoctorReport>`. Update all internal create*Check calls to pass `t`.

For each create*Check function in this batch: add `t: Translator` as first or last parameter (be consistent — recommend last to match builder-pattern conventions). Replace string literals with t() calls.
Be careful with plural ternaries: `${count === 1 ? 'has' : 'have'}` → split into two keys `.singular` / `.plural` and select at caller.
Be careful with variable interpolation: `${stable_id}` → `t('doctor.check.X.message', { stable_id })`. Convert all numbers to strings explicitly: `String(count)`.
DO NOT touch line ranges outside L2087-3034 in this task — TASK-03/04 handle those.

Caller in packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts must now pass translator from getDoctorTranslator(projectRoot) — make that change in TASK-05, but verify in this task that runDoctorReport's call sites are findable (grep `runDoctorReport(`).

If TASK-05 hasn't yet wired CLI doctor.ts to pass translator, this task's intermediate state will compile but throw at runtime when doctor command runs. ACCEPTABLE intermediate state — wave-3 closure resolves it. To avoid breaking tests, EITHER (a) provide a default detectNodeLocale-based translator in runDoctorReport when t is undefined for back-compat during the wave, OR (b) keep this task's diff strictly to the 26 sites + thread translator, and accept that CLI doctor command typechecks but functional-tests will need TASK-05 to land first. Choose (b) — cleaner state machine; doctor command tests in cli package will fail until TASK-05.

After per-task edits: run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server typecheck` to verify thread of translator parameter is sound. `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` may show fallback-to-key-string output (expected pre-TASK-05).

reference.pattern: Existing rc.20 doctor.cite.* and cli.doctor.* keys in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts:140-200 establish naming pattern. Translator threading precedented by other server services that accept t in function signature.
reference.examples: rc.20 TASK-07 (doctor.cite.* keys) added cite-coverage subcommand i18n — same naming scheme reused here at doctor.check.* level.

rationale.chosen_approach: Thread translator through runDoctorReport → create*Check chain. Single-batch sequential commit keeps the diff reviewable. Strict line range scoping (L2087-3034) prevents accidental touching of L3793+ which belongs to TASK-03.
alternatives_considered: Migrate all 84 sites in one task — rejected: single mega-commit hurts bisect granularity and review surface
Use a module-level translator singleton in doctor.ts — rejected: hides the projectRoot dependency, harder to test
decision_factors: Line-range bucketing matches natural file layout
Translator threading is the standard pattern in server services
rationale.tradeoffs: Intermediate state — CLI doctor command produces key-strings as output until TASK-05 lands. Documented in implementation step 11.

manual_checks: typecheck passes after threading translator through ~26 functions
doctor.test.ts may need test fixtures updated to pass a stub translator (use vi.fn() returning key verbatim)
success_metrics: Zero hardcoded English strings remain in L2087-3034 region (grep for common phrases like 'is missing', 'Run `fab' should return zero matches in that range)
All affected create*Check functions accept Translator parameter
unit: doctor.test passes with stub translator returning key verbatim
doctor.test.ts test fixtures updated to provide translator

criteria: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L2087-3034 grep -c 't(' returns ≥ 26 (translator calls present in batch range)
packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts contains import statement for `Translator` from `@fenglimg/fabric-shared`
packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L808 runDoctorReport signature contains exact substring 't: Translator' OR 'translator: Translator'
packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L2087-3034 grep -c '"Bootstrap anchor"' OR '"Agents metadata"' OR '"Event ledger"' (hardcoded English names) returns 0
Commit msg: 'feat(rc26): doctor i18n batch 1 — bootstrap/foundation (TASK-02)'
